topics.name: 100 Level: Legal Methods, Nigerian Legal System, and Use of English
topics.description: LAW 101 Legal Methods, LAW 111 Nigerian Legal System, GST 111 Use of English.

subtopics.name: LAW 101: Legal Methods and Reasoning at UNILAG
objectives: Apply common law rules of statutory interpretation including the literal, golden, and mischief rules
Write legal essays, case analyses, and problem-question answers in standard UNILAG law format

subtopics.name: LAW 111: Foundations of the Nigerian Legal System
objectives: Explain the historical development of Nigerian law from pre-colonial to post-independence era
Identify the hierarchy of courts in Nigeria and jurisdiction of the Supreme Court, Court of Appeal, and High Courts

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Constitutional Law, Contract, Torts, Criminal Law & Property
topics.description: LAW 201 Constitutional Law, LAW 211 Contract, LAW 221 Torts, LAW 301 Criminal Law, LAW 311 Land Law.

subtopics.name: LAW 201 & LAW 211: Constitutional Law and Contract
objectives: Analyse separation of powers and federalism under Nigeria's 1999 Constitution (as amended)
Identify formation, terms, breach, and remedies for contract disputes under Nigerian law

subtopics.name: LAW 301 & LAW 311: Criminal Law and Property Law
objectives: Explain criminal liability, defences, and penalties under the Criminal Code Act and Penal Code
Distinguish freehold and leasehold estates, co-ownership, and land registration under the Land Use Act

topics.name: 400–500 Level: Commercial Law, Evidence, ADR, Clinic & Bar Preparation
topics.description: LAW 401 Company Law, LAW 411 Evidence, LAW 421 ADR, LAW 501 Clinical Legal Education, LAW 599 Law Project.

subtopics.name: LAW 401 & LAW 411: Company Law and Law of Evidence
objectives: Explain incorporation, management, and winding-up of companies under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA 2020)
Apply rules of admissibility, burden of proof, and judicial notice under the Evidence Act 2011

subtopics.name: LAW 501 & LAW 599: Law Clinic and Research Dissertation
objectives: Provide supervised legal advice to clients in UNILAG Law Clinic and draft basic legal documents
Conduct original legal research and defend a dissertation before the UNILAG Faculty of Law board
